mnemonics: BASE Properties: Basically Available, Soft state, Eventual consistency - think of a BASEball game; it's available to watch (available), the score is soft and can change (soft state), and the final result is consistent only at the end (eventual consistency).
keyPoints: NoSQL databases are non-relational and designed for large-scale data storage and heavy workload.
They are schema-less, providing flexibility in data models (document, key-value, column-family, graph).
They follow the CAP theorem (Can only guarantee two: Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance) and BASE model.
commonMistakes: Assuming NoSQL means 'no SQL' and completely avoiding query languages; many NoSQL databases have their own query syntax.
Using NoSQL for all data needs without considering the importance of ACID transactions for financial or critical systems.
Believing that NoSQL databases do not require any schema design; while flexible, schema design is still crucial for performance.
